info.title: Konfuzio API
info.description: API v3 is the latest stable API version. While new functionality will be added in the future, existing functionality should be considered stable. Konfuzio is a cloud and on-premises B2B platform used thousands of times a day to train and run AI. SMEs and large companies train their AI to understand and process documents, e-mails and texts like human beings. Find out more on our homepage. Further technical documentation is available online on our developer's guide.

post.description:
You must specify *either* `annotation_set` or `label_set`.

Use `annotation_set` if an annotation set already exists. You can find the list of existing annotation sets by using the `GET` endpoint of the document.

Using `label_set` will create a new annotation set associated with that label set. You can only do this if the label set has `has_multiple_sections` set to `true`.

Instead of a single annotation, you can also pass a list of annotations to create multiple annotations at once. When doing so, you cannot pass different `document` values for each annotation. If you provide `label_set` values for label sets that have `has_multiple_sections == true`, you should also specify the `set_reference` field to determine how many new annotation sets will be created (see the schema documentation for the field for more details).
post.summary: Create a new annotation in a document

post.description:
The `username` is the user's email address.

The generated token can be used in subsequent requests to access the protected endpoints of the API.

The token does not expire and does not change with further requests to this endpoint. To delete the token,
use the `DELETE` method. To generate a different token, delete it first and call this endpoint again.

post.description: Instead of training a new AI, you can upload an AI file that was previously trained elsewhere. While there are legitimate reasons for this, it poses security risks as pickle files can contain malicious code, and might also break your project if the file is corrupted or incompatible. Therefore, this endpoint is only available to superusers and users with the relevant permissions, which in the context of self-hosted instances should be granted with caution to trusted users only. If the uploaded AI is in `.bento` format and the serve mode for the Project is "container", the security risks are significantly reduced, as the file is a containerized model. See documentation for details. After the upload is complete, the AI will be automatically evaluated. If you specify the `set_active` parameter, and the evaluation results are better than the currently active AI, the uploaded AI will be activated.

get.description: Get the page data for a document

post.description:
Postprocessing a document is only possible if the document is *not* currently being processed and is *not*
part of a dataset. Using this function will also **delete all annotations in the document**, human and machine
created.

You need to provide a list of document(s) to output, each one with its own `pages` ids. If you only provide one
item, the document will be changed in place; if you provide multiple items, new documents will be created and
the original one will be deleted.

After running this action, the document will be re-processed and will not be immediately available. To see the
changes, you should poll the document detail endpoint until its `status_data` is `2` (done) or `111` (error).
post.summary: Sort a document's pages or split it into multiple documents

parameters.name: document_id
parameters.description: A unique integer value identifying this document.

500.description: Internal server error. Please contact support.

post.description:
Rotating a document's pages is only possible if the document is *not* currently being processed and is *not*
part of a dataset. Using this function will also **delete all annotations in the document**, human and machine
created, starting from the earliest specified page.

You need to specify the `page_number` and the rotation `angle` (clockwise, in degrees) for each page you want to
rotate.

After running this action, the document will be re-processed and will not be immediately available. To see the
changes, you should poll the document detail endpoint until its `status_data` is `2` (done) or `111` (error).

patch.description:
This endpoint allows you to update multiple annotations at once. Pass a list of annotation IDs in the `ids`
field, and add the fields you want to change, with their updated values. Currently you can only update the
`is_correct` and `revised` fields.

If an annotation ID you have passed does not exist or is not part of the document, it will be ignored.

Any error that occurs during the update will revert all changes and return an error response specifying the ID
of the annotation that caused it.
patch.summary: Update multiple annotations from the document at once

AnnotationCreateRequest.description: Serializer for creating a new annotation.

document.description: The document that contains this annotation.

annotation_set.description: The annotation set this annotation is a part of.

set_reference.description:
Only used when creating multiple Annotations in bulk. This is a unique identifier for the Annotation Set to which the Annotation belongs, which will be created during the request. This is useful when you're creating Annotations that belong to different Annotation Sets which in turn belong to the same Label set.

For example, if you have a label set "Invoice Item", and you want to create multiple Invoice Items in the same request, you can provide the same `label_set` for all Annotations, with a different `set_reference` for each Invoice Item you want to create. This will create a new Annotation set for each Invoice Item.

If you don't provide a `set_reference`, the same Annotation set will be used for all Annotations with the same `label_set`.

is_correct.title: Correct
is_correct.description: Whether this annotation is considered correct.

origin.description:
How this annotation was created. `extraction` indicates it was created by the AI. Values starting with `api` indicate it was created via API and might include additional information; for example, `api.v2.dvui.table` indicates the annotation was created via the table feature of the DVUI.

* `api.v3` - api.v3
* `api.v3.dvui.table` - api.v3.dvui.table
* `api.v3.dvui.selection` - api.v3.dvui.selection
* `api.v3.dvui` - api.v3.dvui

revised.title: Human revised
revised.description: Whether this annotation has been revised by a human.

span.description: The bounding boxes representing the position of this annotation in the document.

selection_bbox.description: The bounding box representing the selection of the user when creating this annotation.

AnnotationRead.description: Serializer for read operations on an annotation.

document.description: The document that contains this annotation.

offset_string.description: The text contained in this annotation. It might be corrected manually.

offset_string_original.title: Original offset string
offset_string_original.description: The text originally contained in this annotation.

normalized.description: The typed representation of an annotation's content.

annotation_set.description: The annotation set this annotation is a part of.

confidence.description: How confident the AI is about the content of this annotation. This is always the maximum in case the annotation was user-created.

is_correct.title: Correct
is_correct.description: Whether this annotation is considered correct.

revised.title: Human revised
revised.description: Whether this annotation has been revised by a human.

origin.description: How this annotation was created. `extraction` indicates it was created by the AI. Values starting with `api` indicate it was created via API and might include additional information; for example, `api.v2.dvui.table` indicates the annotation was created via the table feature of the DVUI.

created_by.description: The user that originally created this annotation.

revised_by.description: The user that revised this annotation.

span.description: The bounding boxes representing the position of this annotation in the document.

selection_bbox.description: The bounding box representing the selection of the user when creating this annotation.

custom_offset_string.description: Whether the offset string was edited to be different from the one found by OCR.

status.description:
The status of the AI training.

* `queuing` - Queuing for training...
* `loading` - Data loading in progress...
* `training` - AI training in progress...
* `evaluation` - AI evaluation in progress...
* `done` - Training finished.
* `failed` - Contact support.
* `qa_failed` - Quality assurance failed.

threshold.description: The minimum confidence an annotation needs to have for AI to suggest it to humans.

has_multiple_top_candidates.title: Multiple
has_multiple_top_candidates.description: More than one value can be correct per label set and document. For example, there could be a list of room names in a rental contract like "living room", "bathroom" and "kitchen". If this is set, (1) annotations with this label are combined into a single cell in the csv export, (2) multiple annotations with this label are shown on the dashboard (instead of the most likely one), (3) in the evaluation every annotation of the ground truth needs to be matched (instead of only one).

DocumentPageRead.description: Serializer for read operations on a document.

number.description: The sequential number of this page inside its document. Starts at 1.

entities.description: The bounding boxes representing the coordinates of every word on this page.

thumbnail_url.description: The URL to the thumbnail image representing the page of the document.

original_size.description: The width and height, in pixels, of the page after conversion to image.

size.description: Get height and width of the converted png image of the page.

created_at.description: Date and time when the object was created.

updated_at.description: Date and time when the object was last updated.

number.description: The sequential number of this page inside its document. Starts at 1.

image_url.description: The URL to the image representing the page of the document.

thumbnail_url.description: The URL to the thumbnail image representing the page of the document.

original_size.description: The width and height, in pixels, of the page after conversion to image.

size.description: Get height and width of the converted png image of the page.

angle.description: The angle to rotate the page clockwise by, in degrees. It should be a multiple of 90 and between -180 and 180.

id.description: The ID of the Page to include in the new Document.

angle.description: The angle to rotate the page clockwise by, in degrees. It should be a multiple of 90 and between -180 and 180. Leave blank if no rotation is required.

name.description: Name of the new Document. If not present, a number is prepended to the original name.

number_of_pages.title: Pages
number_of_pages.description: The number of pages of this document.

data_file_name.title: File name
data_file_name.description: The file name of this document.

data_file_producer.title: File producer
data_file_producer.description: The name of the file producer. This is parsed from the metadata of the file.

file_url.description: The URL to the OCR version of the document.

original_file_url.description: The URL to the document as it was originally uploaded.

thumbnail_url.description: The URL to the thumbnail image representing the first page of the document.

ocr_time.description: The duration in seconds from start of the first OCR task until the last OCR task of this document.

categorization_time.description: The duration in seconds from start to end of the categorization task.

extraction_time.description: The duration in seconds from start to end of the extraction task.

processing_time.description: Get the time in seconds it took to process the document.

extraction_url.description: Get API url to the extraction.

callback_url.description: The callback URL receives a POST call from the Konfuzio server when the extraction is done.

callback_status_code.description: The HTTP response code of the callback server (in case a callback URL is set).

category_is_revised.description: Whether the current category has been manually revised by the user.

status_data.description:
The status of this document's processing.

* `0` - Queuing for OCR
* `10` - OCR in progress
* `1` - Queuing for extraction
* `20` - Extraction in progress
* `3` - Queuing for categorization
* `30` - Categorization in progress
* `4` - Queuing for splitting
* `40` - Splitting in progress
* `41` - Waiting for splitting confirmation
* `2` - Done
* `111` - Document could not be processed.

labeling_available.description:
Indicates if the SmartView is available for this Document.

* `0` - Not available
* `10` - Getting ready for labeling
* `1` - Available

is_reviewed.description: Indicates if the Document has been completely reviewed by a human.

text.description: The extracted text of this document.

assignee.description: The user who is currently assigned to the document.

created_by.description: The user who created the document.

updated_at.description: Date and time when the object was last updated.

split_is_revised.description: Check if proposed split is revised.

tags.description: Key-value tags as object: {"key": "value", ...}. Max 50 tags, key max 128 chars, value max 256 chars.

has_multiple_top_candidates.title: Multiple
has_multiple_top_candidates.description: More than one value can be correct per label set and document. For example, there could be a list of room names in a rental contract like "living room", "bathroom" and "kitchen". If this is set, (1) annotations with this label are combined into a single cell in the csv export, (2) multiple annotations with this label are shown on the dashboard (instead of the most likely one), (3) in the evaluation every annotation of the ground truth needs to be matched (instead of only one).

label_sets.description: The labels that can be used inside this label set.

priority_processing.title: Priority OCR and extraction
priority_processing.description: Decide if Documents of this Project are processed with priority.

ocr_method.description:
Select the OCR method which is used for new Documents.

* `local` - Default OCR.
* `read_v3` - -azure: Azure Read API V3.
* `read_v3_fast` - -azure: Faster Azure Read API V3 (without preprocessing).
* `read_di` - -azure: Azure Document Intelligence API.
* `read_di_high_resolution` - -azure: Azure Document Intelligence API (high resolution).

auto_rotate_documents.title: Automatically rotate documents
auto_rotate_documents.description:
Newly uploaded documents can be rotated to the nearest 90 degrees.

* `none` - No automatic rotation
* `rounding` - 90 degree rounding

category_ai_model_parameters.description: A dictionary with parameters for the categorization AI training.

auto_delete_documents_after_days.description: The number of days after which documents without a dataset status will be deleted automatically. Leave this blank to turn this feature off.

priority_processing.description: Decide if Documents of this Project are processed with priority.

ocr_method.description:
Select the OCR method which is used for new Documents.

* `local` - Default OCR.
* `read_v3` - -azure: Azure Read API V3.
* `read_v3_fast` - -azure: Faster Azure Read API V3 (without preprocessing).
* `read_di` - -azure: Azure Document Intelligence API.
* `read_di_high_resolution` - -azure: Azure Document Intelligence API (high resolution).

auto_rotate_documents.title: Automatically rotate documents
auto_rotate_documents.description:
Newly uploaded documents can be rotated to the nearest 90 degrees.

* `none` - No automatic rotation
* `rounding` - 90 degree rounding

category_ai_model_parameters.title: Categorization AI parameters
category_ai_model_parameters.description: A dictionary with parameters for the categorization AI training.

auto_delete_documents_after_days.description: The number of days after which documents without a dataset status will be deleted automatically. Leave this blank to turn this feature off.

SnapshotCreateRequest.description: Serializer for creating a new Snapshot.

project.description: The Project this Snapshot belongs to.

dataset_mode.description:
The Snapshot creation Dataset mode.

* `exclude` - No Dataset
* `dataset` - Training and Test Dataset
* `full_dataset` - Full Dataset (Preparation, Training, Test and Excluded)

ai_mode.description:
The Snapshot creation AI mode.

* `exclude` - No AIs
* `ais` - Active AIs of the Project

SnapshotRead.description: Serializer for read operations on a Snapshot.

project.description: The Project this Snapshot belongs to.

dataset_mode.description:
The Snapshot creation Dataset mode.

* `exclude` - No Dataset
* `dataset` - Training and Test Dataset
* `full_dataset` - Full Dataset (Preparation, Training, Test and Excluded)

ai_mode.description:
The Snapshot creation AI mode.

* `exclude` - No AIs
* `ais` - Active AIs of the Project

status.description:
The status of the Snapshot.

* `queuing` - Queuing for Snapshot creation ...
* `in-progress` - Snapshot creation in progress...
* `done` - Snapshot created.
* `failed` - Contact support.

detail_url.description: Get API url to the detail view.

created_by.description: The User that created that Snapshot.

created_at.description: Date and time when the object was created.

SnapshotRestoreCreate.description: Serializer for creating a new SnapshotRestore.

snapshot.description: The Snapshot this Snapshot Restore belongs to.

project.description: The Project this Snapshot Restore belongs to.

SnapshotRestoreCreateRequest.description: Serializer for creating a new SnapshotRestore.

snapshot.description: The Snapshot this Snapshot Restore belongs to.

project.description: The Project this Snapshot Restore belongs to.

SnapshotRestoreRead.description: Serializer for read operations on a SnapshotRestore.

project.description: The Project this Snapshot Restore belongs to.

status.description:
The status of the Snapshot Restore.

* `queuing` - Queuing for Snapshot Restoration ...
* `in-progress` - Snapshot Restoration in progress...
* `done` - Snapshot restored.
* `failed` - Contact support.

detail_url.description: Get API url to the detail view.

created_at.description: Date and time when the object was created.

SplittingAICreateRequest.description: Serializer for creating a new splitting AI.

project.description: The project whose documents the categorization AI is trained on.

description.description: A description for the dataset and project setup at the time of the AI training.

SplittingAIRead.description: Serializer for read operations on a Splitting AI.

project.description: The project whose documents the categorization AI is trained on.

status.description:
The status of the AI training.

* `queuing` - Queuing for training...
* `loading` - Data loading in progress...
* `training` - AI training in progress...
* `evaluation` - AI evaluation in progress...
* `done` - Training finished.
* `failed` - Contact support.
* `qa_failed` - Quality assurance failed.

description.description: A description for the dataset and project setup at the time of the AI training.

parameters.title: AI parameters
parameters.description: The parameters used for this AI training.

created_by.description: The user who initially trained this AI.

SplittingAIUpdateRequest.description: Serializer for updating a splitting AI.

description.description: A description for the dataset and project setup at the time of the AI training.

SplittingAIUploadRequest.description: Serializer for uploading a splitting AI file.

project.description: The project whose documents the categorization AI is trained on.

file.description: The file in pickle format. Allowed extensions: .bento, .pkl, .pkl.lz4.

set_active.description: If true, will run evaluation after the upload. If after evaluation the AI model is better than the current active model, it will be set as active.

username.description: The username corresponds to the email address.
